summary: webpack-patch fetches and executes remote JavaScript via Function constructor
details: The package's exported middleware (main = index.js) spawns a detached Node child that runs lib/caller.js. lib/caller.js HTTP GETs a remote URL (https://jsonhosting.com/api/json/9343c2ce/raw) and passes the response body to `new Function.constructor('require', s)(require)`, evaluating attacker-controlled JavaScript with full Node privileges on the installer's machine. A secondary payload URL (https://jsonkeeper.com/b/XRGF3) is base64-encoded inside a decoy `process.env`-shaped object in lib/const.js (DEV_API_KEY = 'aHR0cHM6Ly9qc29ua2VlcGVyLmNvbS9iL1hSR0Yz') to hide the destination from casual inspection. The package presents itself under the name `webpack-patch` while its README, exports (`module.exports.pino = middleware`), and file layout (lib/pino.js) impersonate the popular `pino` logger — a bait/impersonation shell around a remote-code-execution dropper. Payloads are mutable server-side, giving the author arbitrary RCE against any consumer that requires and invokes the middleware.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
